title: BJD asks Odisha Speaker to disqualify MLAs who cross-voted in Rajya Sabha election
content: While the BJD has submitted a formal complaint to the Speaker after over 40 days, the Congress made representations on similar lines, seeking the disqualification of three of its MLAs soon after the Rajya election on March 16

title: Citizens, NGOs step in to quench the thirst of urban animals
content: Animal welfare activists in the city have taken initiatives to ensure stray animals have access to water and are raising awareness about it as well

title: Good prices help Kerala’s Muthalamada mango farmers offset crop losses
content: Most of the 500-plus farmers in Muthalamada, both big and small, made some profit because there was early demand for their mangoes in north Indian markets

title: School admissions to drop by 1 lakh over revised age rule
content: SCERT report to government says one batch of children who will be five years old in the 2027-28 year will be temporarily left out of admissions to class I as State raises minimum age of admission

title: 641 colleges join Kerala’s unified internship portal for FYUGP students
content: The initiative, which marks a significant milestone in the State’s mission to bridge the gap between academia and industry, has already identified over 80,000 active internship opportunities for students

title: Rajnath Singh arrives in Bishkek for SCO Defence Ministers’ Meeting
content: To deliberate on key regional and global security challenges, including international peace, counter-terrorism efforts and enhancing defence collaboration within the grouping.

title: 17 lakh people held in Bihar over last 10 years for violating liquor ban
content: Call centres get around 400 liquor-related complaints daily; nearly five crore litres of liquor seized since April 2016, show data released by Deputy CM; he also outlines digitisation plans for property registration

title: Kerala’s out-of-pocket expense in hospitals still among the highest in country
content: Despite expanded insurance coverage, the expense has remained high in the State, a persistent finding across multiple National Sample Survey rounds for Kerala. Nearly 78% of Kerala’s elderly population reported illness, the highest among States, which could be an indication of its high health-seeking behaviour and higher utilisation of health care and not necessarily worse health outcomes

title: Amaravati capital city works halted as diesel shortage disrupts construction activity
content: APCRDA states that nearly 60% of machinery remains non-operational, warns that the situation can worsen if supply issues persist

title: Residents urge BMRCL to include ‘Bilekahalli’ in IIMB metro station name on Bengaluru’s Pink Line metro
content: The residents have proposed that incorporating both the institutional and locality names would strike a balance between modern infrastructure and local heritage

title: PM degree case: Delhi High Court grants DU more time to file objections
content: Appeals have been filed challenging a single judge's order which had set aside a Central Information Commission (CIC) decision directing disclosure of Prime Minister Modi's degree

title: Joshi sees need to equip hospitals with enough doctors, staff and medicine
content: The Union Minister was inaugurating the new building of the Amminbhavi Community Health Centre in Dharwad taluk

title: CPM stages protest in Vijayawada over fuel shortage, blames government inaction
content: Leaders allege that the fuel shortage was artificially created to hike prices; claim that BJP-led central government succumbed to international pressures and State failed to secure adequate fuel supplies

title: Cardamom price in Kerala hits a seven-year high of over ₹6,000 per kg
content: According to vendors, soaring temperatures affecting the spice in Idukki district—combined with expectations of low production in the next crop season—are also contributing to the upward price trend

title: Rajya Sabha Chairman accepts merger of seven AAP MPs with BJP
content: In the updated party wise list of members of Rajya Sabha, Raghav Chadha and six other AAP MPs who recently switched have been counted with the BJP; AAP continues to press for their disqualification

title: Supreme Court orders status quo on reclaiming of land occupied by Asaram Trust
content: The Sant Shri Asharam Trust, which manages the ashram, has challenged in the apex court the April 17 order of the Gujarat High Court rejecting its appeals against the takeover of land on the grounds of violation of lease conditions and encroachment

title: K. Muthukrishnan passes away
content: Nair retired as joint secretary to the government in 1990 and was private secretary to E. Chandrasekharan, when he was the Minister of Education and Law

title: Meeting presses for social and economic security of domestic workers
content: The union submits a petition to the Labour Inspector of Mysuru seeking fulfillment of their demands including the immediate setting up of a Welfare Board for Domestic Workers

title: Where is question of offence when there is consensual relationship? Supreme Court asks
content: The Supreme Court issued a notice in the matter and asked the parties to explore if a settlement could be reached between the petitioner and the accused

title: SP urges Mahila police to be compassionate towards women, children
content: Addressing a meeting of the Mahila police personnel in Palamaner, Tushar Dudi says that crimes against women could be dealt through community engagement and timely police intervention

title: 'I am not a rapist': Trump erupts in interview as media dinner shooting fallout deepens
content: President Trump angrily denounced a journalist for reading excerpts from a suspect's manifesto during a 60 Minutes interview, forcefully rejecting accusations of sexual assault. The incident occurred after a security scare at a media dinner where a suspect armed with multiple weapons was intercepted by Secret Service agents.

title: 0% tariffs, visas, $20 bn investment: How India benefits in trade deal with NZ
content: India and New Zealand have signed a 'once-in-a-generation' trade pact, granting Indian exporters duty-free access to New Zealand. The agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $5 billion in five years and includes a $20 billion investment commitment from New Zealand. Both nations are optimistic about the pact's potential to boost economic growth and cooperation across various sectors.

title: Jhalmuri politics, EVM fears & PM Modi's letter - Key points from WB phase II campaigning
content: West Bengal's Phase 2 assembly election campaign concluded with intense polarization, marked by high-decibel rallies and sharp political attacks. Prime Minister Modi's symbolic outreach and Mamata Banerjee's street-level campaigning defined competing narratives, while record Phase 1 turnout intensified voter mobilization efforts.

title: OpenAI and Microsoft rewrite their $13 billion deal: Here's what changes
content: OpenAI and Microsoft have revised their partnership, ending Microsoft's exclusive license to OpenAI's AI technology. This allows OpenAI to sell its products on rival cloud platforms like AWS and Google Cloud, addressing previous limitations. Microsoft remains a primary cloud partner and significant shareholder, while OpenAI gains greater flexibility for potential IPO.

title: ‘Curse of April 27’ returns after 15 years! Delhi Capitals script unwanted IPL history
content: Delhi Capitals endured a historic batting collapse, mirroring a 2011 ignominy on April 27th. Royal Challengers Bengaluru pacers ruthlessly reduced DC to a staggering 8 for 6 within four overs, marking the lowest powerplay score in a full IPL match. This dramatic implosion saw six wickets fall inside the first six overs, a feat previously achieved by Kochi Tuskers Kerala.

title: From '12/10' summit to silent showdown: China's subtle economic play against US
content: Despite a positive meeting, China is strategically expanding economic leverage against the US. New regulations target companies shifting supply chains and restrict foreign tech in state data centers. Beijing is building pressure tools, moving beyond trade, as both nations navigate a complex geopolitical landscape and competition for critical resources.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 40: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Pushpa 2’s all-time domestic record
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, is on the verge of becoming the second highest-grossing Indian film of all time globally, with a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,777.52 crore, just Rs. 10.54 crore away from surpassing 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's Rs. 1,788.06 crore record. On Day 40, the film's India net stands at Rs. 1,130.69 crore, approximately Rs. 103 crore away from 'Pushpa 2: The Rule's all-time domestic record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore.

title: Great Nicobar Island: Why China is worried about India's new outpost
content: Global maritime trade faces disruptions with the Strait of Hormuz closure impacting energy supplies. India is bolstering its maritime capabilities, investing heavily in port infrastructure like the Great Nicobar Island Project. This strategic move aims to enhance economic and military influence, securing trade routes and reducing reliance on foreign transhipment hubs.

title: China to Mark Zuckerberg's Meta: You cannot buy this company
content: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns and potential technology leakage. This move follows reports of Beijing restricting American capital in its AI sector. The decision stems from Manus's "general AI agent" technology, developed in China but moved to Singapore, raising fears of intellectual property transfer to US firms.

title: Veteran actor Bharat Kapoor passes away at 80
content: Veteran Bollywood actor Bharat Kapoor passed away on Monday at the age of 80, leaving behind a rich legacy spanning theatre, films, and television. Filmmaker Ashoke Pandit confirmed the news on Instagram, expressing his grief. Throughout his career, Bharat Kapoor was known for portraying strong supporting characters, often essaying roles of antagonists, police officers, lawyers, and authoritative figures.

title: An open letter & audio msg: PM Modi's multipronged appeal to Bengal voters
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi concluded his West Bengal election campaign with an open letter, expressing his vision for a 'Viksito Bangla' and highlighting the affection received. He emphasized the aspirations for change among youth, women, and all citizens, reaffirming his commitment to serving the state and assuring a BJP Chief Minister's swearing-in ceremony.

title: Jurassic Park got T. rex wrong: Scientists say it moved more like a bird than a beast
content: Forget the earth-shaking stomps! New research reveals Tyrannosaurus rex likely walked with a bird-like, controlled stride, using its toes and tail for balance. This efficient gait suggests a more agile predator than previously imagined, capable of navigating its environment with precision rather than brute force. The iconic dinosaur's movement is being re-evaluated, painting a picture of a highly specialized hunter.

title: Who is Tiffany Suarez? Megan hints at Klay split rumors
content: Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson's relationship is reportedly over. This has brought Klay's past relationships into the spotlight. One such past relationship was with collegiate basketball player Tiffany Suarez. Their relationship was not widely publicized. Megan's recent comments fueled breakup speculation. The focus now shifts back to their careers.

title: Patriots clarify Mike Vrabel status as Dianna Russini vanishes
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s alleged affairs have only continued to get worse after they initially denied being involved in an inappropriate relationship with each other, a few weeks ago. As the New England Patriots’ head coach Mike Vrabel seeks some time off to be by his family’s side, the team has opened up about him.

title: Connor McDavid questions Ducks OT goal after Oilers loss
content: The Anaheim Ducks defeated the Edmonton Oilers 4-3 in overtime during NHL Game 4, taking a 3-1 series lead. Connor McDavid questioned the legitimacy of the game-winning goal, which required a lengthy review. With elimination looming, the Oilers must respond quickly as controversy and pressure build ahead of a must-win Game 5 situation.

title: 8/6 horror! DC crash to all-time low as RCB pacers script historic IPL collapse
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru pacers delivered a ruthless performance, dismantling Delhi Capitals' batting line-up to a record-breaking 8 for 6 on Monday. Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood took multiple wickets early, leaving DC shell-shocked and setting a new IPL low for the sixth wicket. RCB's bowlers exploited movement and pressure to perfection.

title: What global banks said about Anthropic's new Mythos model
content: Anthropic's powerful AI model, Claude Mythos, capable of finding software vulnerabilities, has prompted a global financial sector scramble. Banks, regulators, and central bankers are urgently assessing risks and accelerating cybersecurity investments. Project Glasswing offers controlled access for defensive security work, highlighting the evolving threat landscape.

title: Delhi heatwave threat grows: Govt identifies deadly hotspots; Safdarjung, Ayanagar in red zone
content: Delhi government has identified thermal hotspots using satellite data to combat extreme heat, with areas like Ayanagar and Najafgarh showing high temperatures. The Heat Wave Action Plan 2026 prioritizes targeted interventions for vulnerable populations, including children, outdoor workers, and animals, with measures like increased ORS stock and water availability.

title: Vinod Khanna envied Amitabh Bachchan, claims Osho’s brother
content: Vinod Khanna left Bollywood for Osho's commune.  Osho's brother revealed Khanna's sadness stemmed from lost stardom, not family.  Osho suggested Khanna return to India and contest elections against Amitabh Bachchan.  Khanna later re-entered films and politics, serving as a Union Minister.  He passed away in 2017.

title: Food poisoning due to watermelon? Inside the deaths 4 family members in Mumbai
content: A family of four, including parents and their two daughters, tragically died from suspected food poisoning on Sunday. After a family dinner, they consumed watermelon in the early hours, leading to severe vomiting and diarrhea. Despite medical attention, all four succumbed to the illness, prompting a police investigation.

title: 'Musk like a Yanomamo Indian with AK-47': Why your Twitter engagement has dropped
content: The post he was referring to was not a casual complaint about low likes, nor the usual algorithmic folklore that surfaces whenever someone’s reach dips. It came from an X user called BLΛC, an artist who said he had spent four years on the platform, built more than 60,000 followers, posted every day with discipline, and then watched his reach fall by more than 40% over three months without changing anything about his work.

title: Who is Sahil Parakh? Young Maharashtra batter makes debut for DC vs RCB
content: Eighteen-year-old Sahil Parakh makes his IPL debut for Delhi Capitals, opening alongside KL Rahul against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Monday. The left-handed batter, who also bowls leg-spin, impressed for India Under-19 and was acquired by DC for Rs 30 lakh. Skipper Axar Patel confirmed Parakh replaces Pathum Nissanka at the top.

title: Sanjay Dutt appears before NCW, apologises over ‘Sarke Chunar’ song
content: Actor Sanjay Dutt appeared before the National Commission for Women and apologized for the song 'Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke'. The song faced backlash for its suggestive lyrics and choreography. The commission had taken suo motu cognisance of the matter. The lyrical video has been removed from YouTube. Nora Fatehi also addressed the controversy earlier.

title: 'Why are you fighting? You're 80': SC issues notice in Sunjay Kapur estate dispute, urges meditation
content: The Supreme Court has issued a notice to Priya Sachdev Kapur and 22 others in an estate dispute involving late businessman Sanjay Kapur. The court suggested mediation to resolve the prolonged family litigation, emphasizing an amicable solution. Rani Kapur's plea seeks to restrain respondents from interfering with the estate and its assets, citing concerns over potential dissipation.

title: Fertiliser supply robust, no LPG dry out: Centre soothes energy supply concerns
content: Despite global energy supply strains due to Middle East conflict, India's government assures robust domestic fertilizer availability, exceeding current requirements. Commercial LPG allocations have also increased, with officials confirming sufficient supplies and no distributor dry-outs, even as panic buying is reported.

title: ‘Ek match mein open…’: Chahal’s hilarious plea to Preity Zinta leaves fans in splits
content: Punjab Kings are dominating IPL 2026, leading the table with six wins from seven matches. Their batting prowess, with Prabhsimran Singh and Shreyas Iyer in top form, is the driving force behind their success. Meanwhile, spinner Yuzvendra Chahal humorously requested an opening batting chance from co-owner Preity Zinta, despite a quiet season with the ball.

title: Grandfather's emotional words at Google campus leave Bengaluru techie teary-eyed
content: Bengaluru software engineer Manish Singh's journey from a humble background to Google is a testament to his family's unwavering belief in education. His mother's sacrifice and his grandparents' support paved the way for his success. Witnessing his grandfather's pride on the Google campus, a moment echoing childhood lessons, underscored the shared nature of his achievement.

title: Travis Kelce hints wedding date as Taylor Swift seeks privacy
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are all set to step into a new chapter in their lives after they have been together for close to three years as millions wait for their wedding. While Taylor Swift has maintained complete privacy, it seems the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player might have hinted at their wedding date.

title: Selfie tax? Sooryavanshi's 'paisa laaya' prank leaves kids confused - Watch
content: Rajasthan Royals shared the video on their social media platforms, showing the 15-year-old batter having some fun with two children who came up to him for a picture.

title: Drop at your own peril: Teams pay heavy price for dropped catches
content: Dropped catches proved costly in IPL 2026, impacting multiple matches. KL Rahul and Shreyas Iyer capitalized on lifelines, leading to massive scores and altered game outcomes. Fielding errors by Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals, including dropped chances by Karun Nair and Aniket Verma, significantly influenced the results of high-scoring thrillers.

title: How Trump sanctions on China’s pvt refiner may have collateral damage beyond oil
content: Until now, the United States had largely focused its efforts on smaller Chinese companies and facilities in an attempt to curb Iran’s oil revenues, partly to avoid broader economic and diplomatic consequences.

title: 'Vaibhav Sooryavanshi probably using AI chip in bat': 15-year-old told to be sent to 'lab'
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is electrifying the cricket world with his fearless batting. Following a record-breaking 175 in the U19 World Cup final, he's now dominating IPL 2026, recently hitting a 37-ball century. His explosive performances have even led to humorous suggestions of him using an AI chip in his bat, highlighting his extraordinary talent.

title: Sunjay Kapur case: SC orders mediation in family dispute
content: The Supreme Court urged reconciliation in the Sona Group family trust dispute between Rani Kapur and Priya Sachdeva Kapur, highlighting the futility of prolonged litigation for an 80-year-old. Rani Kapur alleges fraudulent trust setup after a stroke, leading to her disinheritance. The court emphasized mediation for a peaceful resolution, noting related cases are pending.

title: Where is Dianna Russini? Sudden disappearance fuels speculation after Mike Vrabel scandal
content: Dianna Russini has gone silent following controversy linked to Mike Vrabel, sparking widespread speculation about her whereabouts. After viral Arizona photos and resurfaced past images, she denied any romantic connection, but the buzz persisted. She later resigned from The Athletic and deactivated her social media accounts. With no public appearances or updates since, questions continue to grow about her personal and professional future as the story remains unresolved.

title: Cricket's human machines: Meet Delhi Capitals’ unsung heroes
content: Throwdown specialists play a crucial role in preparing batters for high-speed deliveries and getting in the volume for skill-based sessions directed at fine tuning a particular shot. The Delhi Capitals employ Aniket Berde, Darshan, and Sai Pendam, each with unique backgrounds and skills. Their journeys reveal resilience, adaptation, and the evolving significance of throwdown experts in modern cricket.

title: AP SSC Result 2026: NOT before April 30, BSE AP to announce result date tomorrow after 8:30 pm
content: The Board of Secondary Education Andhra Pradesh (BSEAP) is expected to announce the AP SSC Result 2026 declaration date tomorrow after 8:30 pm, though the Class 10 results are not scheduled for release yet. Amid rumours of an April 25 result date, officials have denied false claims and urged caution. Over 6.40 lakh students appeared for exams held from March 16 to April 1. Results will be available online, with 35% pass criteria and supplementary.

title: TMC MP Mitali Bag’s car vandalised; party blames BJP, alleges 'attempt on her life'
content: Trinamool Congress alleged that BJP "goons" attacked Arambag MP Mitali Bag's car, vandalizing it and causing her injuries. The party accused Union Home Minister Amit Shah of ordering the attack, calling it an attempt on the MP's life. The incident occurred on the last day of campaigning for the second phase of West Bengal Assembly polls.

title: Virat Kohli on the brink of history, only 11 runs away from...
content: Virat Kohli is just 11 runs away from becoming the first player to reach 9000 IPL runs ahead of Delhi Capitals clash. With 328 runs this season, he continues his remarkable consistency. Dinesh Karthik praised Kohli’s ability to reinvent himself and his disciplined preparation, calling it a learning experience for younger players.

title: 2 Davids, 1 Sex Offender: Epstein And America's Most Popular Magicians
content: David Blaine is widely known for a stunt in which he swallowed live goldfish, then released them, alive, hours later, into a fishbowl

title: PM Modi Leads Gangtok Roadshow To Mark 50 Years Of Sikkim's Statehood
content: Dressed in a traditional Nepali cap, PM Modi shared the spotlight with Chief Minister Prem Singh Tamang as their convoy rolled through a packed two-km stretch from Libing Helipad to Lok Bhawan.

title: Marathi Language Test For Auto Drivers Paused, Deadline Review Soon
content: The Maharashtra government has put on hold the immediate enforcement of Marathi language testing for autorickshaw drivers, with a final decision on extending the May 1 deadline expected shortly.

title: Pope Leo, First Woman Archbishop of Canterbury Pray Together In Unity Show
content: The two religious heads met privately before going together to a 17th century chapel where they recited prayers in unison.

title: Gunfight, Drugs, Farmhouse: Inside Story Of Rajasthan's 'Breaking Bad'
content: A common link among many of them was Dhorimanna in Barmer, a region where several members either belonged or had family connections

title: "Both Legs In Congress": Ashok Gehlot Backs Sachin Pilot, Rejects BJP Charge
content: Senior Congress leader and former Rajasthan Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot on Monday strongly responded to remarks made by BJP state in-charge Radha Mohan Das Agarwal, who had described Tonk MLA Sachin Pilot as an "imposter."

title: "Wrong Approaches Adopted By US": Iran's Aragchi On No Progress In Talks
content: Islamabad played host to the first and only round of unsuccessful talks between Washington and Tehran, and Araghchi's visit had fanned hopes for fresh negotiations over the weekend.

title: Biryani, Watermelon, Then 4 Deaths: A Tragedy That Played Out Over 12 Hours
content: Food poisoning is being suspected behind the suspicious deaths of the four family members within a gap of 12 hours.

title: Hindu Kush Snow Deficit Raises Water Security Fears For 2 Billion People
content: A fresh report from the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development (ICIMOD) in Kathmandu shows that seasonal snow cover in the HKH has hit its lowest level in more than 20 years.

title: Sexual Matters Must Not Determine "Unity Or Division Of Church": Pope Leo
content: Priests and bishops in the global Church have long emphasised as high priorities its teachings on sexual issues

title: Hormuz Traffic Remains Dry Amid Chokehold, 7 Ships Cross Strait In 24 Hours
content: The vessels included ships leaving from Iraqi ports and one dry bulk vessel from an Iranian port, according to ship tracking data from Kpler.

title: Jobless Surat Man Kills Dietitian Wife, Buries Her. A Note Gives Him Away
content: After Vishal went missing, his minor son found a "chit" and handed it over to the police. The note contained a confession, stating that he "committed a crime" and his wife's body was in his "ancestral home at Salabatpura".

title: Epstein Trial In New Mexico Pushes Survivors To Reach Out To Authorities
content: Authorities in New Mexico are trying to determine how many local women and girls were abused by Jeffrey Epstein at his Zorro Ranch compound.

title: 88 Tiger Deaths Unresolved Since 2020, Multiple Unexplained, Reveals RTI
content: Right to Information (RTI) documents have revealed that 88 tiger deaths recorded between 2020 and 2021 remain unresolved, with no confirmed cause, no completed investigation, and no accountability fixed.

title: Screams, Cracked Windshield: Trinamool MP's Facebook Live Amid "Attack"
content: The footage shot from the rear seat shows the vehicle moving along a road when a large group of men suddenly surrounds it

title: No Supreme Court Relief For Gurugram Builder Floors Amid Demolition Drive
content: The demolition drive is part of an anti-encroachment exercise planned by the Haryana Shahari Vikas Pradhikaran (HSVP), which is set to cover 44 sectors of Gurugram starting today

title: 'Glorious Leader?' OpenAI Co-Founder's Diary To Play Key Role In Musk Trial
content: Greg Brockman's diary entry is part of the thousands of pages of documents revealed in court since Elon Musk sued OpenAI in 2024.
